serving.natural_language_portion: One standard fish burger weighing approximately 180 g, comprising a toasted white bun, a breaded deep-fried fish fillet, lettuce, a slice of processed cheese, and tartar sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Fish burger — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F05020042 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this source. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry 21117 (fish sandwich with tartar sauce, SR Legacy) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for fish burger as a composite dish. GI estimated at 66 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate component: white wheat burger bun (GI ~71, University of Sydney GI Database) modulated downward by the breaded fish fillet, cheese, tartar sauce, and lettuce components which slow gastric emptying. Glycemic load per serving (180 g) calculated as: (GI 66 × available carbohydrate per serving 49.0 g) / 100 = 32.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 541 mg/100 g; a standard 180 g serving delivers approximately 974 mg sodium with negligible potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid fish burgers due to the high sodium content (541 mg/100 g) contributed by tartar sauce, processed cheese, and seasoned breading; if consumed, request no tartar sauce and pair with potassium-rich foods to partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate 27.22 g/100 g with low dietary fibre 1.27 g/100 g; estimated medium GI from white bun and breaded coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should consume fish burgers sparingly because the white bun and breaded coating provide rapidly digestible refined carbohydrate (27.22 g/100 g) with minimal fibre (1.27 g/100 g), producing a medium-to-high glycaemic response; substituting a wholemeal bun and requesting a grilled unbreaded fillet can meaningfully reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 3.2 g/100 g, high sodium 541 mg/100 g, deep-fried preparation with potential trans fat from frying oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit fish burgers because deep-frying the fish fillet increases saturated and potentially trans fat content, the high sodium (541 mg/100 g) elevates blood pressure risk, and the processed cheese and tartar sauce add further saturated fat; choosing a grilled fish fillet on a wholemeal bun with no added sauce is a substantially lower-risk alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation promotes advanced glycation end-product (AGE) formation; high sodium worsens inflammatory tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried fish burgers because AGE formation during frying is pro-inflammatory, high sodium (541 mg/100 g) exacerbates tissue swelling, and the absence of meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients makes this a net inflammatory food; a grilled fish fillet on a wholemeal bun with no tartar sauce is a preferable alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A fish burger is a Western-style fast-food sandwich consisting of a deep-fried breaded white-fish fillet served between toasted white burger buns, typically accompanied by shredded lettuce, a slice of processed cheese, and tartar sauce. The fish fillet is coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crisp, producing a crunchy exterior with a moist, flaky fish interior. The overall flavour profile is savoury and mildly tangy from the tartar sauce, with a soft bun contrasting the crisp coating. Per 100 g the dish provides 277 kcal, 10.73 g protein, 13.93 g fat, and 27.22 g carbohydrate with only 1.27 g dietary fibre, reflecting the refined-carbohydrate-dominant composition of the white bun and breaded coating. Sodium is high at 541 mg/100 g, driven by the tartar sauce, processed cheese, and seasoned breading. The deep-fried preparation method generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and may introduce trans fats from partially hydrogenated frying oils. Clinically, the fish burger is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (high sodium), type 2 diabetes (high refined carbohydrate, low fibre, medium GI of 66), cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, sodium, deep-frying), and rheumatoid arthritis (AGE formation, pro-inflammatory profile). It is widely consumed as a fast-food meal or quick lunch option across Singapore and internationally.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020042, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry 21117 (fish sandwich with tartar sauce, SR Legacy) as a close analogue. GI is an analogous estimate based on the white bun GI anchor from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed data for this composite fish burger dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F05020042) or USDA FoodData Central entry 21117 at the level of precision required, and no other approved source provides these values for this specific composite preparation: monounsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractionation not reported by HPB for this composite dish; polyunsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractionation not reported by HPB for this composite dish; trans_fat_g — trans fat not separately reported by HPB for this composite dish, though trace amounts from frying oil are plausible; potassium_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; calcium_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; iron_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; cholesterol_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_c_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_a_mcg_rae — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_b6_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_b12_mcg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; folate_mcg_dfe — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_d_mcg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; vitamin_e_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; phosphorus_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; magnesium_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; zinc_mg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; selenium_mcg — not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; water_g — moisture content not reported by HPB Foodo for this item. Sources consulted and confirmed unavailable for these fields: HPB Singapore Foodo (F05020042) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 21117. Saturated fat 3.2 g/100 g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry 21117 (fish sandwich with tartar sauce, SR Legacy) as the closest approved-source analogue; this adaptation is documented in nutrient_source. GI value of 66 is an analogous estimate anchored to white wheat burger bun GI (University of Sydney GI Database) and modulated for composite dish composition; no direct GI measurement for fish burger exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. Halal flag set to true as fish is permissible under Islamic dietary law and no pork-derived ingredients are identified in the standard formulation; individual restaurant preparations may vary. The input record listed gi_value as 0 and gi_level as null from an 'Embedded GI Database' — this has been replaced with a defensible analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database anchor per Veritas source policy.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Fixed g3_numeric_hygiene failure by expanding the notes field to individually document every per_100g field set to -1, stating the specific reason data is unavailable for each field, and citing the approved sources (HPB Singapore Foodo F05020042 and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 21117) that were consulted and confirmed unavailable for those values. This satisfies the G3 negative sentinel values documentation requirement.
Iteration 3: Corrected glycemic_load_per_serving from 11.9 (per-100g reference) to 32.3 (per 180 g serving) to align with the gi_source notes which state GL per serving = (66 × 49.0) / 100 = 32.3.
